name: Excitebike
description: Excitebike is a side-scrolling racing game centered around motocross. The player controls a lone rider competing against several AI opponents. The core gameplay involves navigating a series of challenging tracks filled with obstacles such as ramps, bumps, and mud pits. The player's primary goal is to finish in a qualifying position to advance to the next race or complete a track within a certain time in the Design Mode. A key mechanic is the bike's temperature gauge. Overuse of the turbo (speed boost) causes the engine to overheat, temporarily disabling the bike and slowing the player down significantly. The player must carefully manage the turbo and utilize cooling zones to avoid overheating. Collisions with opponents or obstacles result in the rider being dismounted from the bike, requiring the player to manually return the rider to the bike, costing valuable time. The game features a Design Mode allowing the player to create custom tracks using a variety of available track elements. The game has an overhead camera that follows the bike on a side scrolling view. The AI riders compete with each other, also dismounting if they crash. There is only one player in a race, with the other participants being AI players.
